reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 2 (Trust Boundary) triggered. The finding is factually correct: IPC transport bypasses JWT authentication (auth.rs:105-110 starts IPC server without AuthLayer). However, this requires local access to the Unix socket. Per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json trust_assumptions.engine_api, Engine API is for local EL-CL communication and classified as SEMI_TRUSTED. The 01e property (outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W4B5_1771748579.json:453-469) has bug_bounty_eligible=false and bug_bounty_scope='conditional'. Attack scenario requires compromised local process/container to access IPC socket, which falls outside the primary threat model (P2P network attacks). While a valid implementation gap, it requires local privilege escalation as a precondition.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Engine API communication is JWT-authenticated; EL data is semi-trusted but must be verified. (forall engine_api_call: jwt_authenticated(call) && verify_data(call.response))
